title: Seattle Japanese Garden
content: # Immerse yourself in the tranquility and beauty of our exquisite garden. ## Welcome TO Seattle Japanese Garden. Winding gravel paths and stone benches invite you to view the garden slowly and mindfully, in all of its detail - rock, water, lanterns, bridges, buildings, plants and animals. ### Visit in each season to appreciate the full beauty of the garden. The slow transformation of spring to summer to autumn connects us with nature and reminds us of the beauty and impermanence of life. ### Enjoy a perfect visit to the garden by planning ahead. ### Preserve the beauty and tranquility of the garden, please read our policies here. ### Free guided tours are offered from April to October. Our dedicated volunteer guides look forward to sharing the history and horticulture of the garden with you. ### Enrich your experience by attending one of our many educational programs and festivals. Japanese gardening workshops to celebrations of seasonal Japanese traditions, there's something for visitors of all ages to enjoy.

title: Washington Park Arboretum – Garden Review | Condé Nast Traveler
content: See the gardens in bloom. Particularly in spring when Azalea Way is in full bloom and in fall when the Japanese Garden is ablaze with color, the arboretum is a gorgeous place for picnicking, kayaking, bird watching, and just wandering around the gardens. Watching waterfowl from the floating docks on Union Bay, and collecting fallen leaves in the Japanese Garden are my favorite things to do in the arboretum. The Pacific Connections Interpretive Center shelter was built as a community art project using fallen trees from the park. You don't have to be the most active to enjoy the arboretum. $$$ | United States, Seattle, 99 Union St. By most any measure, Seattle’s top hotel. $$ | United States, Seattle, 411 University St. Built in the 1920s, this massive Italian Renaissance hotel is breathtakingly opulent. $$ | United States, Seattle, 2000 4th Ave. Belltown is back, and this Scandinavian-chic stay is a sanctuary from the downtown core.
